topStories.title: US Stock Market: AI spending surge drives Alphabet, Amazon to tap global bond markets
topStories.summary: Alphabet and Amazon are expanding their global borrowing to fund the escalating costs of the AI race. Alphabet plans its first yen-denominated bond sale, while Amazon will tap the Swiss franc market for the first time. These moves reflect the massive investments required for AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

topStories.title: PM Modi doesn't want you to buy gold for next 1 year. A bigger crash on the cards?
topStories.summary: Prime Minister Modi urged Indians to avoid buying gold for a year, a move seen as an attempt to protect foreign exchange reserves amidst global uncertainty and rising crude oil prices. While jewellery stocks saw a knee-jerk reaction, experts believe the appeal may cause temporary caution rather than alter India's deep-rooted affinity for gold.

topStories.title: Assam swearing-in LIVE: Himanta Biswa Sarma to take oath as CM

topStories.title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE Updates: TVK MLA JCD Prabhakar Elected Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker
topStories.summary: Vijay's oath ceremony, which took place on Sunday, came after four days of protracted political negotiations after his party received an unprecedented mandate that split open the binary politics that Tamil Nadu has been witnessing for over 50 years

topStories.title: US Stock Market: BofA, Goldman push back Fed easing forecasts amid inflation risks
topStories.summary: Major Wall Street brokerages have delayed their expectations for U.S. Federal Reserve rate cuts as high energy prices and a strong labour market keep inflation concerns elevated. BofA now expects no rate cuts in 2026, forecasting easing only in mid-2027, while Goldman Sachs has pushed its expected start of rate cuts from September to December 2026.

topStories.title: Tamil Nadu, Kerala govt news LIVE: Vijay issues big liquor order in TN; still no Kerala CM pick by Congress
topStories.summary: Tamil Nadu, Kerala government news LIVE: The Congress has still not announced its Kerala chief minister pick even after a week of assembly election results in which the party-led UDF alliance won a decisive mandate. In TN, new CM Vijay has ordered the closure of over 700 TASMAC liquor stores.

topStories.title: PVR Inox shares slide 6% in two days despite strong Q4 earnings. Do Motilal Oswal, Nuvama see any upside?
topStories.summary: PVR INOX shares dipped despite a return to profitability in the March quarter, reporting a net profit of Rs 187 crore. Strong performance from blockbuster films supported earnings recovery, with revenue rising 26% year-on-year. Analysts remain divided, with some maintaining 'Buy' ratings and others a 'Neutral' stance, citing content pipeline and occupancy sensitivities.

topStories.title: Turning Defensive! Why bond markets are repricing risk amid global pressures
topStories.summary: Fixed income markets face complexity as global and domestic forces necessitate a defensive stance, particularly on duration.  The RBI's ability to lower rates is constrained by external pressures, leading to a widening gap between policy and market yields.  Bandhan AMC has shifted to an underweight duration position, reducing exposure to long-duration government securities.

Global Market: Investors return to emerging markets in April as debt flows surge
topStories.summary: Global investors returned strongly to emerging markets in April, with $58.3 billion in inflows after March’s $66.2 billion outflow, driven mainly by debt markets. According to IIF, easing geopolitical tensions and stabilising conditions restored risk appetite, though concerns remain over energy costs and whether the rebound is sustainable.

topStories.title: ​Goldman Sachs picks Paytm, Swiggy, 10 more stocks for medium-term investors. Do you own any?

topStories.title: Quadria-backed Nobel Hygiene said to plan $300 million India IPO

The company has begun preliminary discussions with investment banks regarding a potential listing. The proposed IPO may involve both new share issuance and secondary sales by existing investors.

topStories.title: Why market is falling today? Sensex falls 700 points as rupee weakens to record low amid Mideast woes. 6 key factors
topStories.summary: Indian stock markets opened lower as the rupee hit a fresh lifetime low, influenced by elevated oil prices and US President Trump's rejection of Iran's peace proposal. The Sensex and Nifty extended losses, with IT stocks among the top decliners, reflecting broad bearish sentiment amid global uncertainties and FII selling.

topStories.title: Silver gains Rs 3,100/kg, gold at Rs 1.54 lakh/10g ahead of US inflation data, fading peace talk hopes. What should you do?
topStories.summary: Gold and silver prices rose on MCX Tuesday as investors turned cautious ahead of key U.S. inflation data. Weakening hopes of a U.S.-Iran peace deal lifted crude oil prices, fuelling concerns that persistent inflation could keep global interest rates higher for longer.

topStories.title: IHCL shares in focus after Q4 results; firm highlights Iran-US war impact. Here’s what Morgan Stanley, other brokerages say

business.summary: Four MLAs of the NDA will also be sworn in at a function in the presence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union ministers and chief ministers of BJP-ruled states.

business.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay orders closure of 717 TASMAC outlets near temples, schools and bus stands
business.summary: The government said the move is part of a broader effort to regulate liquor outlet locations and ensure compliance with distance norms from sensitive public spaces

business.title: US Stock Market: AI spending surge drives Alphabet, Amazon to tap global bond markets
business.summary: Alphabet and Amazon are expanding their global borrowing to fund the escalating costs of the AI race. Alphabet plans its first yen-denominated bond sale, while Amazon will tap the Swiss franc market for the first time. These moves reflect the massive investments required for AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

business.title: PM Modi doesn't want you to buy gold for next 1 year. A bigger crash on the cards?
business.summary: Prime Minister Modi urged Indians to avoid buying gold for a year, a move seen as an attempt to protect foreign exchange reserves amidst global uncertainty and rising crude oil prices. While jewellery stocks saw a knee-jerk reaction, experts believe the appeal may cause temporary caution rather than alter India's deep-rooted affinity for gold.

business.title: US Stock Market: BofA, Goldman push back Fed easing forecasts amid inflation risks
business.summary: Major Wall Street brokerages have delayed their expectations for U.S. Federal Reserve rate cuts as high energy prices and a strong labour market keep inflation concerns elevated. BofA now expects no rate cuts in 2026, forecasting easing only in mid-2027, while Goldman Sachs has pushed its expected start of rate cuts from September to December 2026.

business.title: CBI files seventh chargesheet in homebuyers fraud case
business.summary: Following the complaint, the agency laid a trap and allegedly caught the Computer Operator red-handed while demanding and accepting the bribe amount on behalf of the Superintendent, CGST Meerut

business.title: JCD Prabhakar elected speaker of 17th Tamil Nadu Assembly unopposed
business.summary: M Ravishankar was elected as Deputy Secretary unopposed

business.title: PVR Inox shares slide 6% in two days despite strong Q4 earnings. Do Motilal Oswal, Nuvama see any upside?
business.summary: PVR INOX shares dipped despite a return to profitability in the March quarter, reporting a net profit of Rs 187 crore. Strong performance from blockbuster films supported earnings recovery, with revenue rising 26% year-on-year. Analysts remain divided, with some maintaining 'Buy' ratings and others a 'Neutral' stance, citing content pipeline and occupancy sensitivities.

business.title: Turning Defensive! Why bond markets are repricing risk amid global pressures
business.summary: Fixed income markets face complexity as global and domestic forces necessitate a defensive stance, particularly on duration.  The RBI's ability to lower rates is constrained by external pressures, leading to a widening gap between policy and market yields.  Bandhan AMC has shifted to an underweight duration position, reducing exposure to long-duration government securities.

business.title: Global Market: Investors return to emerging markets in April as debt flows surge
business.summary: Global investors returned strongly to emerging markets in April, with $58.3 billion in inflows after March’s $66.2 billion outflow, driven mainly by debt markets. According to IIF, easing geopolitical tensions and stabilising conditions restored risk appetite, though concerns remain over energy costs and whether the rebound is sustainable.

business.title: ​Goldman Sachs picks Paytm, Swiggy, 10 more stocks for medium-term investors. Do you own any?
business.summary: ​Goldman Sachs has identified twelve stocks for investors looking at the medium term. These companies are expected to perform well as foreign investor sentiment improves. The list includes prominent names like Paytm and Swiggy. Foreign ownership in these selected stocks has decreased, and they are trading at attractive valuations.

business.title: Quadria-backed Nobel Hygiene said to plan $300 million India IPO
business.summary: Nobel Hygiene, a hygiene products manufacturer backed by Quadria Capital, is exploring an initial public offering in India that could raise up to $300 million. The company has begun preliminary discussions with investment banks regarding a potential listing. The proposed IPO may involve both new share issuance and secondary sales by existing investors.

business.title: Why market is falling today? Sensex falls 700 points as rupee weakens to record low amid Mideast woes. 6 key factors
business.summary: Indian stock markets opened lower as the rupee hit a fresh lifetime low, influenced by elevated oil prices and US President Trump's rejection of Iran's peace proposal. The Sensex and Nifty extended losses, with IT stocks among the top decliners, reflecting broad bearish sentiment amid global uncertainties and FII selling.

business.title: Silver gains Rs 3,100/kg, gold at Rs 1.54 lakh/10g ahead of US inflation data, fading peace talk hopes. What should you do?
business.summary: Gold and silver prices rose on MCX Tuesday as investors turned cautious ahead of key U.S. inflation data. Weakening hopes of a U.S.-Iran peace deal lifted crude oil prices, fuelling concerns that persistent inflation could keep global interest rates higher for longer.

business.title: IHCL shares in focus after Q4 results; firm highlights Iran-US war impact. Here’s what Morgan Stanley, other brokerages say
business.summary: The Indian Hotels Company Limited (IHCL) reported a 15% year-on-year rise in net profit to Rs 600 crore for Q4 FY26, despite a 36% sequential decline. Revenue also saw a year-on-year increase but a sequential dip. The company acknowledged the impact of the Iran-US war on its operations, particularly in Dubai.

business.title: Can Reliance Jio's fresh-issue play restore faith in India's billion-dollar IPOs?
business.summary: Reliance Jio may change its IPO plan. Instead of existing investors selling shares, it could be a fresh issue. This means money goes directly into Jio Platforms for debt reduction and expansion. This move could restore investor faith in large Indian IPOs. It might set a new standard for future mega listings. The market will watch this development closely.

business.title: Rupee hits record low of 95.55 as fraying US-Iran ceasefire keeps oil prices on the boil
business.summary: The rupee has reached its lowest point ever against the US dollar. This decline is driven by investor concerns over escalating conflict in the Middle East. The situation has led to a surge in oil prices. This development poses economic challenges for India, which imports most of its energy needs. The rupee's new low reflects these growing worries.

business.title: Can Tata Motors' CV business deliver a profit surprise in Q4 as truck demand picks up?
business.summary: Tata Motors' commercial vehicle business is poised for a strong March quarter, with analysts anticipating healthy volume growth and margin expansion. This recovery is driven by a significant pickup in fleet replacement demand and improved freight activity, reflecting a broader upturn in the domestic trucking cycle.

business.title: Chasing US tech stocks? Aashish Somaiyaa says India and emerging markets look more attractive now
business.summary: Aashish Somaiyaa, CEO of White Oak Capital, believes selective stock picking is key in the current market. He sees India's recent flat performance as a strategic entry point. Global investors should look beyond US tech towards emerging markets. The market faces near-term challenges from geopolitical events and energy prices. Somaiyaa's strategy focuses on identifying individual stock winners.

business.title: Don't go all in right now; Systematix bets on PSU banks, IT, and defence, avoids realty and jewellery
business.summary: Geopolitical tensions necessitate a disciplined, diversified investment approach, according to Systematix Group's Nikhil Khandelwal. He advises a staggered capital deployment over three to six months, emphasizing crude oil and the rupee as key variables. Khandelwal favors PSU banks, IT, and capital goods for their resilience and valuation, while cautioning against real estate, jewellery, and airlines.

business.title: ETMarkets PMS Talk | From Rs 1 crore to Rs 85 crore in 24 years - the power of high-conviction investing, says Alok Agarwal
business.summary: Alchemy Capital Management's Alok Agarwal highlights how a high-conviction, benchmark-agnostic approach has yielded extraordinary long-term wealth.  A ₹1 crore investment in 2002 grew to ₹85 crore by April 2026, significantly outperforming the BSE 500 TRI.  The strategy focuses on identifying structural growth themes and maintaining disciplined exits, proving its mettle over 24 years.

business.title: Mind Over Money | Sameer Mathur: From cricket to pickleball—lessons in anticipation, adaptability, and market success
business.summary: Sameer Mathur, MD of Roinet Solution, shares how cricket and pickleball influence his business approach. He highlights adaptability, quick decisions, and teamwork. Mathur explains how reading changing conditions and balancing speed with precision are key. Building resilient organizations requires agility and trusting the process. Empowering teams creates long-term competitive advantage.
